description: One floating (gantt) bar per category spanning a reference-line measure to a parsed actual score: a template SPLIT calc parses the combined actual score out of a compound '<code> <win>-<lose>' string (e.g. 'PHI 40-22' → 40+22=62), the size calc draws the span (actual − reference line), and an over/under SIGN calc colors each bar over (right/teal) vs under (left/red). Deviation of an actual outcome from a betting/reference line, per member.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the SPLIT parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22'); a different shape parses the wrong combined score
both endpoints are already numeric measures — bind a simpler dual-measure or reference-line deviation chart instead of the string-parse calc

slots.notes: The row/category each floating bar is drawn for (one mark per member).

The reference-line/betting-line endpoint on columns AND the subtrahend of the size and over/under calcs. Derivation 'min' is authored fidelity from the checkpoint — correct only at one-row-per-category grain (see min-derivation-per-row hazard).

Compound-score STRING (e.g. 'PHI 40-22') SPLIT-parsed by the actual-score calc into a combined numeric score (40+22=62). Bindable + required so templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs the bare [Actual Input] token and Gate 6 can prove the dependency is bound. Never placed on a shelf (no column-instance).

calcs.formula: INT( SPLIT( TRIM( SPLIT( [Actual Input], "-", 1 ) ), " ", 2 ) ) + INT( TRIM( SPLIT( [Actual Input], "-", 2 ) ) )
calcs.notes: GOLDEN actual-score parse (Over/Under and Betting Lines golden.twb: Winning Score = INT(SPLIT(TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',1)),' ',2)), Losing Score = INT(TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',2))), Total = Winning + Losing). Replaces the checkpoint's NULL-producing bare INT([Actual Input]) — INT() of a leading-alpha compound string ('PHI 40-22') is NULL, collapsing every bar to zero span. Not shelved; consumed as a template-internal input by the size and over/under calcs.
avoid_when: the actual string is not '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' — the nested SPLIT/TRIM assumes a single-token prefix before the space and a hyphen between winner and loser scores

calcs.notes: Bar length = parsed actual score − reference line. Consumes the template-internal actual-score calc (SPLIT-parsed) and the bindable Reference Value; rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs [Reference Value] on bind, and leaves the template-owned [Calculation_ActualScore] untouched (unmapped).
avoid_when: there is more than one row per category — the 'min' derivation collapses multi-row members to their minimum span

calcs.notes: GOLDEN over/under color = sign(actual − line): +1 over (right/teal #76b7b2), -1 under (left/red #e15759), 0 push. Replaces the checkpoint's mis-chosen raw O/U Winner (Favorite/Underdog spread result) color. Shelved discretely as [sum:Calculation_OverUnder:ok] with a mark palette style-rule so the color splits by true over/under, not the spread.
avoid_when: over/under is not defined against the reference line — this SIGN colors purely by (parsed actual − reference), so a reference that is not the true betting/expected line mislabels the split

hazards.detail: The actual-score calc parses a combined score out of a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' string via nested SPLIT/TRIM (GOLDEN semantics). The parse is dataset-shape-specific: a different string shape (multi-token prefix, no hyphen, non-numeric scores) yields NULL/garbage. Advisory only — surfaced via avoid_when and gated by render_verified, not a hard fast_path_blocker.

The calcs reference bare bindable [Actual Input]/[Reference Value]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them ONLY because both are bindable bare-key slots. The template-internal [Calculation_ActualScore] ref is intentionally left unmapped (a template-owned calc). Gate 6 asserts the bindable deps are bound before injection.

The size calc and the reference line use derivation 'min' (authored fidelity from the checkpoint). Min equals the row value only when there is exactly one mark per Category; a category with multiple rows collapses to the minimum. Bind at one-row-per-category grain or override the derivation.
